content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA with NNN non-negative integers. There are NNN bars arranged adjacent to each other, such that the height of the ithi^{th}ith bar is AiA_iAi​ units and the width of each bar is 111 unit.

Find out the total amount of water the bars can trap after raining.

**Note**: Use 64 bit integers.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers - the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the total amount of water the bars can trap after raining.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N – the number of bars. The second line contains N space‑separated non‑negative integers A1 … AN, the heights of the bars.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total amount of water that can be trapped after raining. Use 64‑bit integer output.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: bars [2,1,1,3] trap 2 units of water.
Test case 2: no lower valley between higher bars, so 0.
Test case 3: only two equal bars, no space for water.
Test case 4: bars [3,0,2,0,4] trap 7 units of water.
The middle bar of height 0 is bounded by two bars of height 2, so it holds 2 units of water.
Water trapped at indices 2,4,6 equals 2,3,1 respectively, total 6.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array from both ends using two pointers left and right. We keep the maximum height seen so far from the left (left_max) and from the right (right_max). At each step we move the pointer whose side has the smaller current maximum because the water level at that side is limited by that maximum. If the current bar is lower than its side's maximum we add the difference to the answer, otherwise we update the maximum. This runs in O(N) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of bars in that test case. Overall O(sum of N) across all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input array.
